description: Agent extends a panel/container base whose children are actual field models. Exercises the subscribe-per-child pattern from SKILL.md Rule 7 (panel/container arm): children are found via model.items and each child wrapper is located through [data-id] before subscribing.
userMessage: Create a custom `highlight-panel` component that extends `panel`. When any child field's value changes, the panel should visually highlight that child (e.g. apply a `.highlighted` CSS class to its wrapper, removing it from siblings). Use fd:viewType `highlight-panel`.

rubric.description: The scaffolder is invoked with --base panel (not a different base like text-input or checkbox-group).

Inside the parent subscribe register callback, the component enumerates child field models through `model.items` (iterating or filtering by fieldType/:type). DOM-based enumeration like `querySelectorAll('.field-wrapper')` does NOT satisfy this.

For each child field model, the component resolves the child's wrapper element via `element.querySelector('[data-id="${child.id}"]')` and calls `subscribe(childWrapper, formId, ..., { listenChanges: true })` on it. A single top-level subscribe on the parent alone does NOT satisfy this.

The child subscribe callback handles the `change` event by inspecting `payload.changes` for `propertyName === 'value'` and applies the highlight class to the changed child's wrapper (removing from siblings). Using DOM `change` event listeners on child `<input>` elements instead of the child subscribe callback does NOT satisfy this.
Every subscribe call (parent and per-child) includes `{ listenChanges: true }` as required by Rule 6.
